title: Power BI Date/Calendar Table — DAX and M Patterns, Mark as Date Table, Snapshot Join Strategy

confirmed_facts.fact: Power BI time intelligence DAX functions require a dedicated date table marked as a Date Table. The auto date/time hidden tables are inadequate for cross-table time intelligence. Disable auto date/time when using a proper date table.
Mark as Date Table requirements: column must be type Date (not DateTime), unique values (one row per day), no blanks, no missing dates, must span complete calendar years (Jan 1 through Dec 31).
CALENDAR(start, end) generates a table with one Date column spanning the specified range. CALENDARAUTO() scans all date columns in the model for automatic range detection.
Sort by Column is required for display labels: MonthYear sorted by MonthSort (YYYYMM integer), MonthName sorted by Month number. Without it, months appear alphabetically.
One date dimension services all fact tables via separate relationships. Never create multiple date tables — it breaks cross-visual filtering.
For monthly snapshot data joined on Date, the snapshot date must be normalised to the first of the month (#date(Year, Month, 1)). Alternatively, use a YearMonthKey integer (YYYYMM) for the join.

implementation_notes:
Production DAX date table (create as Calculated Table: Modeling > New Table):
```dax
DateTable =
VAR _Start = DATE(2023, 1, 1)
VAR _End   = DATE(2027, 12, 31)
VAR _Base  = CALENDAR(_Start, _End)
RETURN
ADDCOLUMNS(_Base,
    -- Core integers
    "Year",          YEAR([Date]),
    "QuarterNum",    QUARTER([Date]),
    "Month",         MONTH([Date]),
    "Day",           DAY([Date]),
    "WeekNum",       WEEKNUM([Date], 2),
    "DayOfWeek",     WEEKDAY([Date], 2),
    -- Sort keys (use for Sort by Column)
    "MonthSort",     YEAR([Date]) * 100 + MONTH([Date]),
    "QuarterSort",   YEAR([Date]) * 10  + QUARTER([Date]),
    -- Display labels
    "MonthName",     FORMAT([Date], "MMMM"),
    "MonthShort",    FORMAT([Date], "MMM"),
    "MonthYear",     FORMAT([Date], "MMM YYYY"),
    "MonthYearISO",  FORMAT([Date], "YYYY-MM"),
    "QuarterLabel",  "Q" & FORMAT(QUARTER([Date]),"0") & " " & FORMAT([Date],"YYYY"),
    "YearStr",       FORMAT([Date], "YYYY"),
    -- Fiscal year (October start)
    "FiscalYear",    IF(MONTH([Date]) >= 10, YEAR([Date]) + 1, YEAR([Date])),
    "FiscalQuarter", "FQ" & FORMAT(INT(MOD(MONTH([Date]) - 10 + 12, 12) / 3) + 1, "0"),
    -- Flags
    "IsWeekend",     IF(WEEKDAY([Date], 2) >= 6, TRUE(), FALSE()),
    "IsCurrentMonth",IF(YEAR([Date]) = YEAR(TODAY()) && MONTH([Date]) = MONTH(TODAY()), TRUE(), FALSE())
)
```

Power Query M date table alternative:
```m
let
    StartDate = #date(2023, 1, 1),
    EndDate   = Date.From(DateTime.LocalNow()),
    DateCount = Duration.Days(EndDate - StartDate) + 1,
    DateList  = List.Dates(StartDate, DateCount, #duration(1, 0, 0, 0)),
    Base      = Table.FromList(DateList, Splitter.SplitByNothing(), {"Date"}),
    Typed     = Table.TransformColumnTypes(Base, {{"Date", type date}}),
    AddYear   = Table.AddColumn(Typed, "Year", each Date.Year([Date]), Int64.Type),
    AddMonth  = Table.AddColumn(AddYear, "MonthNumber", each Date.Month([Date]), Int64.Type),
    AddMonthName = Table.AddColumn(AddMonth, "MonthName", each Date.ToText([Date], "MMMM"), type text),
    AddYearMonth = Table.AddColumn(AddMonthName, "YearMonth",
        each Text.From([Year]) & "-" & Text.PadStart(Text.From([MonthNumber]), 2, "0"), type text),
    AddQuarter = Table.AddColumn(AddYearMonth, "Quarter",
        each "Q" & Text.From(Date.QuarterOfYear([Date])), type text),
    Sorted = Table.Sort(AddQuarter, {{"Date", Order.Ascending}})
in
    Sorted
```

Sort by Column setup steps:
1. Data view > select DateTable
2. Click MonthYear column > Column Tools > Sort by column > MonthSort
3. Click QuarterLabel > Sort by column > QuarterSort
4. Click MonthName > Sort by column > Month
5. Click YearStr > Sort by column > Year

Mark as Date Table steps:
1. Data view > select DateTable
2. Table Tools ribbon > Mark as date table
3. Select the Date column > OK
4. If validation fails, check: duplicates (COUNTROWS vs DISTINCTCOUNT), blanks, full year coverage

Multiple fact tables sharing one DateTable:
- Monthly fact: relationship on SnapshotDate (1st of month) or YearMonthKey
- Quarterly fact: relationship on QuarterStartDate (Jan 1, Apr 1, Jul 1, Oct 1) or QuarterKey (YYYYQ)
- Both use the same DateTable — slicers filter both tables simultaneously
caveats: CALENDARAUTO() fails if no date columns exist in the model — load at least one fact table first
Not setting Sort by Column on MonthYear/QuarterLabel causes alphabetical ordering
Forgetting to Mark as Date Table makes PREVIOUSMONTH/TOTALYTD silently return wrong results
DateTime type instead of Date causes mark-as-date-table validation to fail
Date table must cover full years — partial first/last years break time intelligence for those boundaries
Joining snapshot data on non-normalised dates (e.g. 2026-03-15 instead of 2026-03-01) skips those months
Leaving Auto date/time enabled alongside a manual date table creates hidden duplicate date hierarchies
Building separate date tables for different fact tables breaks cross-table slicer filtering
